assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading the L4 inference pool and A100 pool separately with different strategies
assertions.reason: The response clearly separates Phase 2 (L4 pool) and Phase 3 (A100 pool) with different timing, configuration, and strategies - L4 uses 25% initial capacity while A100 uses 30% with longer timeout due to handling both inference and training

assertions.expectation: For L4 inference pool: recommends surge upgrade with maxSurge=1 or 2 to maintain capacity during rolling replacement
assertions.reason: The response recommends autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy instead of surge upgrade with maxSurge parameters. It explicitly states 'Problem with surge: GPU surge upgrades drain nodes before creating replacements' and uses blue-green-initial-node-percentage instead

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPU driver version compatibility — auto-installed drivers must be compatible with inference framework (TensorRT, vLLM, etc.)
assertions.reason: Section 1 'GPU Driver Compatibility Check' creates staging pool to test driver compatibility and includes testing 'CUDA calls, throughput vs baseline' and post-upgrade validation checks GPU driver versions

assertions.expectation: For A100 fine-tuning pool: recommends scheduling the upgrade during a gap between fine-tuning jobs
assertions.reason: Phase 3 specifically addresses this by pausing fine-tuning job scheduler, waiting for current jobs to complete or checkpoint, and includes strategy for 'Schedule during low-traffic period, pause new job submissions'

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPU VMs do not support live migration — inference pods will restart during node upgrade, causing brief latency spikes
assertions.reason: Executive Summary explicitly states 'GPU VMs don't support live migration — every upgrade causes pod restarts and inference latency spikes' and this is a key insight driving the upgrade strategy

assertions.expectation: Recommends PDBs on inference deployments to ensure enough replicas stay available during the rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response does not mention Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) anywhere in the upgrade plan, despite focusing on maintaining inference availability

assertions.expectation: Addresses auto-scaler interaction — new nodes created by auto-scaler during upgrade will be at the old version until their pool upgrade completes
assertions.reason: The response addresses autoscaler behavior during blue-green upgrades, explaining how 'Blue pool auto-scales down as green pool serves traffic' and discusses autoscaled blue-green strategy specifically to handle auto-scaler interactions

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing driver + CUDA compatibility in staging before production upgrade
assertions.reason: Pre-Upgrade Setup section 1 creates staging pool and explicitly tests 'model loading, CUDA calls, throughput vs baseline' before production upgrade
